book_claims.mechanism: Exceptionally rich in vitamin C (ascorbic acid), an essential antioxidant and collagen-synthesis cofactor; red peppers also provide pro-vitamin A (beta-carotene) supporting immune function and cellular integrity.
book_claims.text: Red peppers are the top food source of vitamin C (95 mg per 1/2 cup raw, 158% DV) and a significant source of vitamin A (117 mcg RAE per 1/2 cup, 47% DV). Green peppers provide 60 mg vitamin C per 1/2 cup (100% DV). Used in salads and side dishes throughout the Longevity Diet.

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Lobo et al., Pharmacognosy Reviews (2010) — PMID 22228951
supplementary_research.finding: Vitamin C acts as a powerful water-soluble antioxidant, regenerates vitamin E, and participates in collagen biosynthesis — functions directly relevant to reducing oxidative stress, a hallmark of aging.

Adequate vitamin C intake supports both innate and adaptive immune defenses; deficiency impairs neutrophil and lymphocyte function, supporting the Longevity Diet's emphasis on micronutrient adequacy for longevity.
Capsicum compounds (capsaicin) modulate TRPV1 nociceptors and have anti-inflammatory properties; regular consumption associated with reduced inflammatory markers in observational data.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Heat-labile; highest when consumed raw. Even lightly cooked peppers retain ~50–70% of vitamin C.
Fat-soluble; absorption increased when consumed with olive oil or other dietary fat.
Important for amino acid metabolism and neurotransmitter synthesis; stable during moderate cooking.
Anti-inflammatory flavonoids; bioavailability enhanced by fat and slightly degraded by prolonged cooking.

synergies.note: Fat from olive oil dramatically enhances absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids (beta-carotene, lutein) from peppers.
Vitamin C in peppers enhances non-heme iron absorption from chickpeas, improving iron bioavailability in plant-based meals.
Vitamin C from peppers converts ferric iron in spinach to the more absorbable ferrous form, a key longevity-diet strategy for iron adequacy.
